key_insight: WEST is an open-source, massively parallel excited-state simulation code that unifies GW, Bethe-Salpeter, TDDFT, and defect embedding without explicit virtual states to scale to large materials systems.
engineering_relevance: It enables practical, high-fidelity prediction of optical and excited-state properties on thousands of GPUs, supporting materials discovery workflows for systems with more than a thousand atoms.
method: Plane-wave pseudopotential framework with common algorithms for GW, BSE, TDDFT, and quantum defect embedding
Avoids explicit virtual electronic states using density functional and density matrix perturbation theory
Uses low-rank screening/exact-exchange representations, localization, hierarchical parallelism, and GPU acceleration
evidence_signal: The abstract reports representative applications across spin defects, perovskite excitons, and water/ice, with near-ideal strong scaling to thousands of GPUs and simulations of >1000-atom systems.
watch_item: The main implementation concern is the complexity of maintaining accuracy and performance across many coupled methods, backends, and workflow interfaces at large scale.
paper_title: The WEST code for large-scale excited-state materials simulations
